hero_desc: A day of unity and celebration for the African diaspora.
next_event_desc: Join us for our monthly community gathering — a relaxed networking evening for the African diaspora and allies in Barcelona. Entry is free. Drinks and food available from Volta Restaurant & Bar.
next_event_location: 📍 Volta Bar · Hotel Borneta · Passeig de Picasso 26–30, El Born
next_event_series_note: Part of a monthly series on the 19th of each month.
intro_body:
At Juneteenth Barcelona, we believe that the African diaspora deserves a space to gather, celebrate, and be seen. Our mission is to honor the history of Black liberation & creativity while building a living, breathing community in one of Europe's most vibrant cities.

We honor history by celebrating Black culture, and building bridges across the African diaspora living in and visiting the city.
intro_quote: "A city meaningfully connected to the African diaspora — whose stories deserve to be told."
stat2_label: Countries represented in the food hall
wt_body: Trace the visible and invisible footprints of Black diasporic presence through Barcelona — from medieval accounts of African residents, to monuments funded by colonial profits, to the hubs of knowledge open today. A 2–3 hour self-guided walk through the city's hidden history.
